item.dc:creator: Kelly O'Donnell
Peter Nicholas
Corky Siemaszko
item.description: John Bolton, who served as national security adviser during President Donald Trump’s first term and later became one of his fiercest critics, has agreed to plead guilty to one count of retaining national security information, two sources familiar with the matter said

media:content.media:text: John Bolton in Copenhagen.
media:content.media:description: John Bolton in Copenhagen on Sept. 4, 2025.

item.title: Former Trump adviser John Bolton to plead guilty to retaining national security information

item.dc:creator: Sahil Kapur
Frank Thorp V
item.description: The Senate began voting on a bill to fund ICE and Border Patrol as Republicans split on Trump’s $1.8 billion “anti-weaponization” fund.

media:content.media:text: The Capitol Building
media:content.media:description: Senators are in for a long day of votes.

item.title: Senate begins voting on Republican bill to fund ICE as GOP is split on Trump’s $1.8B fund

item.dc:creator: Albinson Linares
item.description: Immigration attorneys are having to take down sites and social media channels as scammers use AI, deepfakes and cloned domains to impersonate them and scam clients.

media:content.media:text: Angel Leal Jr., an immigration attorney practicing in Doral, Fla., whose image, name and even voice was cloned with artificial intelligence to scam immigrant clients.
media:content.media:description: Angel Leal Jr., an immigration attorney practicing in Doral, Fla., whose image, name and even voice was cloned with artificial intelligence to scam immigrant clients.

media:content.media:text: A fake Andrea Jacoski ID used by scammers.
media:content.media:description: Angel Leal Jr., an immigration attorney practicing in Doral, Fla., whose image, name and even voice was cloned with artificial intelligence to scam immigrant clients.

item.title: Immigration lawyers slam scammers stealing their identities and offering fraudulent services

item.dc:creator: Courtney Kube
Julia Ainsley
Dan De Luce
item.description: WASHINGTON — The CIA has put several senior officials on administrative leave over their handling of a high-ranking officer who allegedly had $40 million in gold bars stashed at his home, according to three people familiar with the decisions

media:content.media:text: Gold bars.
media:content.media:description: Court papers say David Rush had requested and received from the CIA a large amount of foreign currency and gold bars (not pictured) for “work-related expenses.”

item.title: CIA puts senior officials on leave over officer arrested with gold bars

item.dc:creator: Francie Ebert
item.description: A mother in Florida is suing the Campbell’s Co.

media:content.media:text: Campbell Soup Co. Products As Earnings Figures Are Released
media:content.media:description: A can of Campbell's SpaghettiOs pasta.

item.title: Woman finds moving worms and parasites in SpaghettiOs, lawsuit says

item.dc:creator: David Cox
item.description: New research reveals a mental workaround that is activated through repetition and experience.

media:content.media:text: Businesswoman talking on smart phone at desk
media:content.media:description: Side view of businesswoman talking on mobile phone while working on computer.

item.title: How the brain rewires itself to juggle multiple tasks

item.dc:creator: Tim Rohan
item.description: The former striker will serve as a World Cup commentator for Telemundo, the tournament’s official Spanish-language network.

media:content.media:text: Jozy Altidore
media:content.media:description: Jozy Altidore, a former Toronto FC player, delivers the match ball before a match between Toronto FC and Inter Miami CF in Toronto on May 9.

media:content.media:text: United States forward Jozy Altidore (17)
media:content.media:description: Jozy Altidore, a former Toronto FC player, delivers the match ball before a match between Toronto FC and Inter Miami CF in Toronto on May 9.

item.title: Jozy Altidore, now a broadcaster, is bullish on the U.S. making a deep World Cup run

item.description: As the World Cups heads to 11 cities across the U.S., states are ramping up security to keep fans safe. New Jersey police take NBC News' Sam Brock inside their command center, where they will monitor stadiums, hotels, and training centers ahead of and during matches.

media:content.media:title: New Jersey police ramp up security as state prepares for World Cup final
media:content.media:description: As the World Cups heads to 11 cities across the U.S., states are ramping up security to keep fans safe. New Jersey police take NBC News' Sam Brock inside their command center, where they will monitor stadiums, hotels, and training centers ahead of and during matches.

item.title: New Jersey police ramp up security as state prepares for World Cup final

item.dc:creator: Jesse Kirsch
item.description: Despite disappointment over seat locations, many supporters remain grateful for access to relatively affordable tickets.

media:content.media:text: A banner for the American Outlaws
media:content.media:description: An American Outlaws banner at a U.S. game against Portugal at Mercedes-Benz Stadium in Atlanta March 31.

media:content.media:text: United States v Belgium - International Friendly
media:content.media:description: An American Outlaws banner at a U.S. game against Portugal at Mercedes-Benz Stadium in Atlanta March 31.

item.title: Team USA’s loudest supporters say FIFA pushed them to upper deck for World Cup

item.dc:creator: Raquel Coronell Uribe
item.description: President Donald Trump said Thursday that his newly appointed acting Director of National Intelligence, Bill Pulte, will not take up the position permanently

media:content.media:text: William Pulte speaks outside
media:content.media:description: A handful of Senate Republicans said they would not back Pulte's confirmation to a permanent position as DNI.

item.title: Trump says Bill Pulte will only serve in an acting capacity as DNI

item.dc:creator: Dareh Gregorian
Kyle Stewart
Gabrielle Khoriaty
Isabelle Schmeler
item.description: The House Oversight Committee said Thursday it was asking the Justice Department to investigate sexual misconduct allegations made by Jeffrey Epstein’s former assistant, Sarah Kellen, against two of his associates

media:content.media:text: Jeffrey Epstein's Former Assistant Sarah Kellen Interviewed By House Oversight Committee
media:content.media:description: Jeffrey Epstein's former assistant Sarah Kellen, right, arrives to testify at a closed-door interview with the House Oversight Committee on Capitol Hill on May 21.

item.title: House committee calls on DOJ to investigate two men with ties to Jeffrey Epstein

item.dc:creator: The Politics Desk
item.description: In today’s edition, Jonathan Allen explores how congressional Republicans are starting to push back on some of President Donald Trump’s wishes.

media:content.media:text: President Trump Makes An Announcement On Coal From The White House's Oval Office
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump speaks during a "Beautiful, Clean Coal" event in the Oval Office of the White House on June 4, 2026.

item.title: Cracks emerge in GOP support for Trump’s agenda: From the Politics Desk

item.dc:creator: Francie Ebert
item.description: Making plans to see Christopher Nolan’s “The Odyssey” turned into a quest of its own for some fans on Thursday as a surge of would-be ticket buyers appeared to bog down AMC’s website

media:content.media:text: Matt Damon is Odysseus in "The Odyssey."
media:content.media:description: Matt Damon is Odysseus in "The Odyssey."

media:content.media:text: Matt Damon and Zendaya in "The Odyssey."
media:content.media:description: Matt Damon is Odysseus in "The Odyssey."

item.title: ‘The Odyssey’ ticket rush overwhelms AMC website and app

item.dc:creator: Courtney Kube
Julia Ainsley
Dan De Luce
item.description: WASHINGTON — The CIA has put several senior officials on administrative leave over their handling of a high-ranking officer who allegedly had $40 million in gold bars stashed at his home, according to three people familiar with the decisions

media:content.media:text: Gold bars.
media:content.media:description: Court papers say David Rush had requested and received from the CIA a large amount of foreign currency and gold bars (not pictured) for “work-related expenses.”

item.title: CIA puts senior officials on leave over officer arrested with gold bars

item.dc:creator: Gary Grumbach
Alexandra Marquez
item.description: Attorneys for the iconic performing arts venue said the signage must revert to previous names like The John F. Kennedy Center for the Performing Arts by June 12.

media:content.media:text: APTOPIX Trump Kennedy Center
media:content.media:description: The John F. Kennedy Center for the Performing Arts in Washington on May 29.

item.title: Kennedy Center lawyers tell staff to remove references to Trump in signage

item.dc:creator: Rohan Nadkarni
Tim Rohan
item.description: The San Antonio Spurs had taken a 1-point lead, with just more than two minutes to play in the fourth quarter of Game 1 of the NBA Finals last night, when something remarkable happened.

media:content.media:text: 2026 NBA Finals - Game One
media:content.media:description: Jalen Brunson of the New York Knicks shoots during game one of the NBA Finals.

media:content.media:text: New York Knicks Fans Gather In Manhattan To Watch Game 1 Of The NBA Finals Against San Antonio Spurs
media:content.media:description: Jalen Brunson of the New York Knicks shoots during game one of the NBA Finals.

media:content.media:text: Mexico's midfielder Gilberto Mora vies for the ball with Australia's defender Aziz Behich during a friendly match in Pasadena, Calif., on May 30, 2026.
media:content.media:description: Jalen Brunson of the New York Knicks shoots during game one of the NBA Finals.

item.title: From The Sports Desk: Jalen Brunson, ‘Captain Clutch,’ ices Game 1 for Knicks

media:content.media:text: The Capitol Building
media:content.media:description: Senators are in for a long day of votes.

item.title: Senate begins voting on Republican bill to fund ICE as GOP is split on Trump’s $1.8B fund

item.dc:creator: Kaitlin Sullivan
item.description: Ticks were found carrying a rare type of Lyme disease in New York. It’s the first time these bacteria were found outside of Minnesota and Wisconsin.

media:content.media:text: A deer tick on a blade of grass.
media:content.media:description: Deer ticks can carry bacteria that cause Lyme disease.

item.title: Rare type of Lyme disease found for the first time in New York

item.dc:creator: Francie Ebert
item.description: Vasana Montgomery has apologized following her removal from the “Love Island USA” cast after videos surfaced that appeared to show her using a racial slur.“I wanted to address a couple of videos from my teen years that have recently resurfaced.

media:content.media:text: Vasana Montgomery in a black bathing suit
media:content.media:description: Vasana Montgomery.

item.title: Booted ‘Love Island USA’ contestant apologizes for past use of racial slur

item.dc:creator: Kelly O'Donnell
Peter Nicholas
Corky Siemaszko
item.description: John Bolton, who served as national security adviser during President Donald Trump’s first term and later became one of his fiercest critics, has agreed to plead guilty to one count of retaining national security information, two sources familiar with the matter said

media:content.media:text: John Bolton in Copenhagen.
media:content.media:description: John Bolton in Copenhagen on Sept. 4, 2025.

item.title: Former Trump adviser John Bolton to plead guilty to retaining national security information

item.dc:creator: David K. Li
item.description: Japanese police trudged through waist-high mud on Thursday in another unsuccessful day searching for a missing Auburn University student, though his parents said they remain optimistic about their son’s potential rescue

media:content.media:text: James Higginbotham.
media:content.media:description: James Higginbotham.

item.title: Another day passes with Auburn student still missing in Japan, parents say

item.dc:creator: Mithil Aggarwal
Matteo Moschella
item.description: Labeled a “massacre” by authorities, video showing migrant workers being burned alive is driving a reckoning over the exploitation of foreign laborers in Italy.

media:content.media:text: Police in Italy arrest 2 people after apparent torching of a car kills 4 farmworkers.
media:content.media:description: Police in Italy arrested two people after the apparent torching of a car in which four farmworkers were killed.

item.title: Video of 4 men burned to death by suspected gangmasters highlights abuse of migrant workers in Italy

item.dc:creator: Zoe Malin
item.description: We’re tracking the best early Amazon Prime Day 2026 deals live, including discounts on tech, home, beauty, kitchen products and more before the four-day sale begins.

media:content.media:text: Amazon has early Prime Day deals on kitchen, tech, home items and more.
media:content.media:description: Amazon has early Prime Day deals on kitchen, tech, home items and more.

media:content.media:text: Amazon has early Prime Day deals on kitchen, tech, home items and more.
media:content.media:description: Amazon has early Prime Day deals on kitchen, tech, home items and more.

item.title: The 51+ best early Amazon Prime Day 2026 deals you can shop now: Bissell, Le Creuset, Levi’s, and more (live updates)

item.dc:creator: Bethany Heitman
item.description: Sandals are notoriously not great for lots of walking. These podiatrists-approved sandals have built-in arch support and cushioning to keep you comfortable.

media:content.media:text: According to podiatrists, the key to a comfy sandal is built-in arch support and thick, secure straps.
media:content.media:description: According to podiatrists, the key to a comfy sandal is built-in arch support and thick, secure straps.

media:content.media:text: According to podiatrists, the key to a comfy sandal is built-in arch support and thick, secure straps.
media:content.media:description: According to podiatrists, the key to a comfy sandal is built-in arch support and thick, secure straps.

item.title: 6 podiatrist-approved sandals to comfortably walk in all summer
